raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("folic acid supplementation start time")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the provided evidence, there's strong consensus emphasizing the importance of initiating folic acid supplementation before conceiving to minimize risks associated with neural tube defects. Studies indicate that supplementation ideally begins five to six months before planned conception, aligning closely with option B "Three months before becoming pregnant." While some sources mention general guidance suggesting any time post-marriage/presumed engagement, the majority stress pre-conception timing due to the rapid nature of neural tube development occurring very early in pregnancy, sometimes before awareness of pregnancy exists. Therefore, I concur with my initial recommendation favoring B ("Three months before becoming pregnant"). My updated stance remains firm as the evidence strongly supports preemptive folic acid administration far exceeding mere engagement/post-wedding timelines specified elsewhere less prominently. Thus, $\boxed{\text{B}}$ still stands best supported by existing literature highlighting pre-pregnancy window significance exclusively.
